subject: Complaint: MRI Claim Denied Without Warning
body: I am filing a formal complaint about the denial of my MRI claim (CLM-MB-003). My PCP referred me to an orthopedic specialist, who ordered the MRI for my right knee. At no point was I informed that a prior authorization was required. I received a bill for $1,950 with no warning. This is unacceptable. I have already called member services and filed an appeal, but I want this complaint on record. The process for communicating prior auth requirements to members needs to be improved.

subject: RE: Complaint: MRI Claim Denied Without Warning
body: Dear Marcus, thank you for reaching out. We understand your frustration and sincerely apologize for the lack of communication regarding the prior authorization requirement. Your complaint has been documented (CASE-MB-001). Your appeal is currently under review and we expect a decision within 30 days. We are also forwarding your feedback about prior auth communication to our quality improvement team. If you have further questions, please call Member Services at 1-800-555-0199. — Janet Rodriguez, Member Services

subject: Unhappy with Ozempic Denial — Step Therapy Policy
body: I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the denial of my Ozempic prescription (AUTH-MC-001). My endocrinologist, Dr. Reyes, specifically recommended Ozempic because my diabetes is not well-controlled on Metformin alone (A1C 7.8%). Instead, I am being forced to try Jardiance first because of a step therapy requirement. I understand there is a process, but I believe my doctor should have the final say in my treatment. I am now starting Jardiance as required, but if it doesn't work or causes problems, I expect the Ozempic approval to be processed quickly. Please document this concern.

subject: Thank you — A1C improvement on Ozempic
body: I wanted to send a quick thank you note. After the frustrating experience with the step therapy denial earlier this year, I am happy to report that my A1C has dropped from 7.8% to 6.9% since starting Ozempic in July. Dr. Reyes and my PCP Dr. Wilson are both pleased with the results. I still think the step therapy process was unnecessarily difficult, but I appreciate the team helping me navigate it. The Jardiance did not work for me (caused UTIs), but I'm grateful the Ozempic was eventually approved. Thank you for your help.

subject: What's covered for delivery at University Hospital?
body: Hi, I'm 38 weeks pregnant and planning to deliver at University Hospital in Portland. I want to make sure I understand what's covered under my plan. Can you confirm: (1) University Hospital is in-network, (2) what my estimated out-of-pocket will be for a vaginal delivery with a 2-night stay, and (3) whether the anesthesia for an epidural is included? This is my first baby and I want to avoid any surprise bills. Thank you!

subject: Formal Complaint: Surprise $4,200 Anesthesia Bill
body: I am filing a formal complaint about claim CLM-SD-014. I delivered my baby at University Hospital on October 5th — an in-network facility that I verified in advance (see my message from September). Despite this, I received a $4,200 bill because the anesthesiologist assigned by the hospital was out-of-network. I had ZERO choice in this. I was in active labor. I specifically asked about coverage beforehand. This is exactly the kind of surprise billing the No Surprises Act was created to prevent. I called yesterday (reference CASE-SD-001) and was told an appeal was filed, but I want this complaint formally documented. If this is not resolved, I will file a complaint with the Oregon Division of Financial Regulation. This has been an incredibly stressful experience during what should be a happy time.
